---
title: "VIS Housing Segregation in Bogotá"
description: "Geospatial and statistical analysis of how Social Interest Housing contributes to urban segregation in Bogotá."
date: "2026-01-01"
categories: [Geospatial, Statistics, Python, Public Policy]
image: ../assets/vis-bogota-preview.svg
---

## Summary

Social Interest Housing (VIS) aims to guarantee access to decent housing for low-income households in Colombia. However, the concentration of these projects in peripheral areas of Bogotá may reinforce socio-spatial segregation patterns. This analysis quantifies that effect using data from the real estate transaction registry (SNR) and spatial statistical methods.

---

## Research Question

> Does the location of VIS projects in Bogotá amplify socio-spatial segregation, or does it help reduce it?

---

## Data

| Source | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| SNR | Georeferenced real estate transactions |
| DANE | Socioeconomic data by UPZ |
| Catastro Bogotá | Neighborhood and UPZ polygons |

---

## Methodology

1. **Cleaning and georeferencing** of SNR transactions with Python (pandas, geopandas)
2. **Segregation indices** (dissimilarity index, exposure index)
3. **Spatial analysis** — spatial autocorrelation (Moran's I), heat maps
4. **Visualization** — interactive maps with Kepler.gl

---

## Main Results

> ⚠️ *This section is under construction. Add the key findings here.*

- Finding 1: ...
- Finding 2: ...
- Finding 3: ...

---

## Visualizations

> ⚠️ *Add screenshots of Kepler.gl maps or other visualizations here.*

---

## Code & Reproducibility

Full analysis available on GitHub:

🔗 [github.com/YeisonBL/vis-segregacion-bogota](https://github.com/YeisonBL/vis-segregacion-bogota)

---

## Publication

This work was developed as part of the **Statistics Specialization, Universidad Nacional de Colombia (2025–2026)** and is available as a preprint.

🔗 *Preprint link (coming soon)*
